Content: Jika betul syg, dia tunggu.Usually LDR yg sakit kalau gini. Ada org jodoh senang, kenal sekejap terus kawin. Ada yg tak, kena tahan lama tuk bersama. It's all about timing.Believe me, bila dh together tu, both of u will appreciate more. Trust in Allah.Kuncinya, "Don't give up so easily."
translated_experience: If it's true, darling, he'll wait. Usually LDRs are sick like this. There are people who are happy soul mates, meet each other for a moment and then get married. There are some who don't have to be together for a long time. It's all about timing. Believe me, if you're together, both of you will appreciate more. Trust in Allah. The key is, "Don't give up so easily."

Content: LDR akan tetap rasa dekat if dua dua beri energy yang sama. My partner gaves me 200% effort every single day without getting tired. Jarak tak sepatutnya jadi isu yang besar/utama if you sedar bahawa kebahagiaan boleh dicorakkan dengan pelbagai cara. At the end of the day, it all depends on diri sendiri nak pilih corak yang macam mana. Sama ada 'hidup' berpaksikan hubungan orang lain, atau put an effort and corakkan hubungan sendiri mengikut kemampuan dan ruang yang hakikatnya ada dalam hubungan.
translated_experience: LDR will still feel close if both give the same energy. My partner gave me 200% effort every single day without getting tired. Distance shouldn't be a big/main issue if you realize that happiness can be shaped in many ways. At the end of the day, it all depends on what kind of pattern you want to choose. Either 'life' centered on other people's relationships, or put an effort and pattern your own relationship according to the ability and space that actually exists in the relationship.

Content: Should I leave him? I'm in a ldr with this man for four years and I think I can't do this anymore. He sent me a paper bag full of chocolate and a lego flower for my first birthday with him. He sent it to me two months before my actual birthday because he 'forgot' my birthday. I forgive him, thinking he got it mixed up with someone else's. That was the first and last time he wished and gave me birthday present. He also frequently left me on read but still watches my stories. I don't feel loved

Content: Mula-mula kami janji nak kuat. Setiap hari video call, setiap malam âgoodnightâ. Tapi lama-lama, semua tu mula pudar. Dia sibuk, aku pun letih.Rindu makin dalam, tapi jarak makin jauh. Aku tengok gambar dia setiap malam, cuma nak rasa âdekatâ walau hakikatnya, aku cuma bercakap dengan skrin.Aku rindukan dia , tapi lebih dari tu, aku rindukan masa di mana semuanya terasa mudah.
translated_experience: First we promise to be strong. Every day video call, every night “goodnight”. But over time, all that started to fade. He's busy, I'm tired too. The longing is getting deeper, but the distance is getting farther. I look at his picture every night, just to feel close, even though the truth is, I'm just talking to the screen. I miss him, but more than that, I miss the time when everything felt easy.

Content: LDRstoryMy husband and I met 8 years ago, never thinking weâd be âthe oneâ for each other. Content:
Not many people celebrate long distance relationships, but today feels like a good day to acknowledge how far weâve come.
This year, our long distance relationship is going into year 3.
Love isnât just about physical presence.
Itâs about choosing to talk even when youâre tired or emotionally drained
Some days feel strong,
some days feel exhausting.
But somehow, we always come back to choosing each other.
To everyone in an LDR,
youâre stronger than you think ð¤

Content: My husband and I have been LD since April 2020âon opposite coasts of North America, 3 hour time difference. Our trips to see each other range from every 6 months, to 4 and now we try to keep it even shorter. To answer your Q, donât depend on a âsparkâ. An open, honest and communicative relationship is incredibly hard work, but so worth it. It becomes a built in best friend kind of companionship. It doesnât fizzle and makes you feel incredibly cherished.

Content: I'm in an LDR of just under a year (UK-based). Usually see each other once a month due to work commitments, sometimes longer when I have a few weeks off in between. Since meeting, we've texted every day, and called a few times a week. Then calls became more and more frequent. It happened organically so whatever works for you. But I would say it's important to keep checking in, ask how their day went, keep asking questions and getting to know them, make sure they have your support and interest...

Content: My guy and I are 110 miles apart. Weâve been together over a year and a half. We FaceTime. Call. Text. Share pics and videos. Sometimes watch a game or movie at the same. We always always know when we will see each other next. Itâs not every weekend so we keep a calendar. We send cards in the mail, too. We talk about missing each other. Weâve met each others friends so I know who heâs with when he goes out with someone and same here. We share a lot and yet each still have a good life.

Content: Most of my relationships as an adult have been LDR. Ranging from 4,200 miles apart to 400 miles apart. Longest was 3 years and that was the 4,200 mile separation. It never bothered me to the point of considering leaving. For me, personally, waiting was a non-issue and no amount of distance would've led me to 'goodbye'. They took me away from themselves. Not the distance.

But after 4 months, I decided to resign and move to be with him. Emotionally, it was really draining. I was juggling house chores and taking care of our son all alone. But now? Weâre so much happier living together again like before. Sometimes, one partner has to make a sacrifice for the well being of the family.

Content:
Been there. Kalau waktu berdua oke je ldr, seronok. Kerja kite habiskan duit dia, berjalan, travel, fly, keluar dengan kawan waktu husband takde.
Tapi start ada anak , we both decide yang tak boleh mcm ni. Kesian dekat anak.
So now, eventho dah takde allowance kepuk2, tapi setiap hari dapat tgk muka depan mata, husband dapat jaga anak sekali. Kerja dia pun tak stress. Rezeki Allah Alhmdulillahð©·
translated_experience:
Been there. If it's time together it's okay ldr, it's fun. My work spends his money, walking, traveling, flying, going out with friends when my husband is not around.
But when we start having children, we both decide that we can't do this. I feel sorry for the child.
So now, even though I don't have an allowance anymore, but every day I can see the front of my eyes, my husband can take care of the children for once. His work is not stressful. God's blessings Alhamdulillah

Content: Not married but I have been with my partner for almost 5 years. I met him just before having to move to the other side of the country. He would come every two months for a week and we always have a particular date in the week just for us. We are both gamers so it is mostly geeking together. Also we try to have a holiday together at least once a year. We are not all on communication, we don't text each other every day but we would often send memes or links to videos we liked.

Content: LDR never really worked for me. Iâm more into simple, random dates â like balik kerja go dinner, pasar malam, teman cari barang, or just lepak tomyam. I value someone actually being there more than just having a relationship label. For me, real presence is important to build a connection. Words of affirmation donât really resonate with me, so verbal reassurance alone isnât enough. I need a partner who shows up and is physically present, acts of service matter more to me.

Content: Mostly peacefulness. Suami mmg bagi rse protected and safe etc but what i appreciate most is ketenangan yg wujud dlm hubungan kami. Tiada "what ifs" or doubts bcs of the trust he built from day one. Dulu selalu ovt macam2, skrg rse mcm "its ok, if its happened, i hv hands to hold and a shoulder to cry on". Walaupun LDR, Alhamdulillah Allah bagi rasa tenang kpd kami.
translated_experience: Mostly peacefulness. My husband feels protected and safe, etc., but what I appreciate most is the calm that exists in our relationship. There are no "what ifs" or doubts because of the trust he built from day one. In the past it was always ovt all kinds of things, now it's like "its ok, if it happens, I have hands to hold and a shoulder to cry on". Even though we are in a LDR, Alhamdulillah Allah has given us peace of mind.

Content:
My partner and i have a 13 hour time difference. We would call any chance we get, have movie dates and stay until one of us falls asleep. If we cant call, we chat, send reels, send pictures or random videos. We send each other care packages too.
I think LDR is the purest kind of love, cause you both love and stay loyal even when you dont see each other everyday. But LDR will only work when both of you make the effort to make it work. Good luck to you both! Remember, LDR wont last forever.

Content:
LDR only works if you both have at least Time, Money and Plan to bridge the LDR gap in the future, hindi pwedeng love lang. You are just scared of the past. Best thing to do is communicate, if pareho kayong willing to work it out then start asap. Express how you feel, your partner will never know everything thats been going on your mind and how you feel.
And please, never ever seek out the things you currently cant get from your partner with someone else.
Hope things get better soon ð

Content:
Stop doing LDR and find a real relationship, based in reality, where you and your partner are supported by network of friends both mutual and separate, that make it less necessary (and less possible) to be avoidant or secretive while ensuring human connection needs are being met.
LDRs have false intensity, smoke and mirrors, real love isnât based on desperate longing or romantic monologues itâs nurtured by shared values and compatibility with being in each otherâs spaces long term.

Content: Long distance love is not for the weak. It tests your patience, your trust, and your emotions on days you really just want a hug instead of a phone call. You miss the little moments, the random pull ups, the quiet nights together. But it also teaches you how to love with intention, communicate with honesty, and appreciate consistency. Distance shows you that real love is not about access, it is about effort. And when it is real, the wait only makes the love stronger. ð¤ð¤ð¿

Content:
Well, my daughters' Dad (aka my ex-husband) lived in the US and I in Denmark. We dated long-distance for almost 4 years before I moved over there and stayed with him for an additional 10 years. This was before internet and cellphones (yeah, I'm old as dirt, lol).
Now, back in Denmark, I just ended a 14 months LDR with someone living in Germany.
There's pros and cons, but if the love and commitment is there, why shouldn't it work?
